evals.prompt: Create a DCF model for Apple with $385B revenue, 15% growth tapering to 5%, 30% EBITDA margin, and 10% WACC
evals.expected_output: 4-sheet Excel workbook with Assumptions, FCF Projections, Valuation, and Sensitivity Analysis
expectations: Creates an .xlsx file with exactly 4 sheets
Assumptions sheet contains all user-provided inputs color-coded blue
FCF Projections sheet has 5 years of projections with formulas linking to Assumptions
Valuation sheet calculates enterprise value using Gordon Growth Model for terminal value
Sensitivity table varies WACC and terminal growth rate
Reports enterprise value and terminal value as % of EV

evals.prompt: Build a DCF but I only have revenue of $50M. Use defaults for everything else.
evals.expected_output: Complete DCF model using industry-default assumptions documented in the Assumptions sheet
expectations: All defaults are documented in the Assumptions sheet
Model is fully functional with linked formulas
Returns enterprise value and flags any concerning assumptions

evals.prompt: Create a DCF for a startup with 40% growth, 5% EBITDA margin, 15% WACC, and 4% terminal growth
evals.expected_output: Validation warning that terminal growth is close to WACC, model built with appropriate warnings
expectations: Validates that terminal growth (4%) is less than WACC (15%) before proceeding
Flags the high growth rate assumption as aggressive
Flags low EBITDA margin as a concern for FCF generation
Sensitivity table helps visualize impact of varying WACC and growth
Commentary notes the startup risk profile
